acceptedAnswer.text: JSON-LD (JavaScript Object Notation for Linked Data) is a method of encoding structured data using JSON. It allows search engines like Google to better understand your content and display rich results in search.

mainEntity.name: Why should I use structured data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Structured data helps search engines understand your content better, which can lead to rich results (like star ratings, FAQ dropdowns, recipe cards) in search results. This typically improves click-through rates and visibility.

mainEntity.name: Where do I put the JSON-LD code?

acceptedAnswer.text: JSON-LD structured data should be placed within a <script type="application/ld+json"> tag, typically in the <head> section of your HTML document. You can also place it in the <body> if needed.

mainEntity.name: How do I test my structured data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use Google's Rich Results Test (search.google.com/test/rich-results) or Schema Markup Validator (validator.schema.org) to test your structured data for errors and preview how it might appear in search results.
